channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Lamar Jackson breaks NFL QB rushing record, accounts for 3 TDs in Ravens' 31-2 romp over Texans
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Lamar Jackson broke the NFL career rushing record for quarterbacks in Baltimore's 31-2 victory over the Houston Texans on Wednesday, bolstering his case for MVP as the Ravens moved closer to the AFC North title.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Embiid shakes off pregame fall, leads Sixers to 118-114 win over Celtics
item.description: Joel Embiid popped up from a pregame fall and scored 27 points with nine rebounds to lead the Philadelphia 76ers to a 118-114 victory over the Boston Celtics.
item.dc:creator: Jimmy Golen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Heat list Jimmy Butler as doubtful for Thursday as speculation about his future intensifies
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The Miami Heat are listing Jimmy Butler as doubtful for their game Thursday at Orlando, saying he needs time to recondition before returning to the lineup. Butler did not fly with the team to Orlando on Wednesday night, and his intentions for future games seem a bit unclear as well.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Edwards scores 26, Wolves hold on to beat Mavs 105-99 after Doncic injury
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Edwards scored 26 points, and the Minnesota Timberwolves held on for a 105-99 Christmas Day victory over Dallas after Mavericks superstar Luka Doncic left the game with a strained left calf.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump floats NHL legend Wayne Gretzky as Canadian prime minister amid Trudeau turmoil
item.description: President-elect Trump expressed his support for former NHL great Wayne Gretzky as prime minister of Canada in a post on social media on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mahomes throws 3 TDs as Chiefs clinch AFC's top seed by breezing past the skidding Steelers 29-10
item.description: Patrick Mahomes passed for 320 yards and three touchdowns and the Kansas City Chiefs locked up the top seed in the AFC for the fourth time in seven seasons with a 29-10 win over the Pittsburgh Steelers.
item.dc:creator: Will Graves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Warriors' Steph Curry gets candid about eventual NBA retirement
item.description: During a recent sit-down with ESPN, 36-year-old Golden State Warriors superstar and four-time NBA champion Steph Curry got candid about his life after basketball.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles Hall of Famer Bill Bergey dies at 79 after lengthy health battle
item.description: On Christmas morning, Bill's son, Jake Bergey, posted the news of his father's passing on social media and described his dad as an "out right great person.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Players have mixed feelings about being on the road on Christmas as NFL adds more holiday games
item.description: OWINGS MILLS, Md. (AP) — Asked if there was any special meaning to playing on Christmas for a second straight season, Lamar Jackson paused for several seconds before answering. That said it all. “I mean, it is,” he finally replied, trying to be diplomatic.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USC's JuJu Watkins is AP women's basketball player of the week
item.description: The Associated Press national player of the week in women’s basketball for Week 7 of the season: JuJu Watkins, USC The sophomore guard shined in the Trojans' 72-70 victory over UConn, scoring 25 points with six rebounds, five assists and three blocks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wemby at The Garden. LeBron vs. Steph. The NBA's Christmas Day lineup, as always, has star power
item.description: LeBron James made his Christmas debut in 2003. Victor Wembanyama was born 10 days later. That's right: James has been featured on the NBA's big day for longer than Wembanyama has been alive.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gary Player is 89 and has a new girlfriend. Nelly Korda has dog sledding in her holiday plans
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Gary Player is a big believer that one can't go through life without love. Even at age 89, he's never too old to find it. Player happily shared his big news at the PNC Championship. He has a new girlfriend. “I said I wanted to live until 100.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bucks roll past Bulls, 112-91, despite being without 2 star players
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — No Dame, no Giannis, no problem for the Milwaukee Bucks on Monday night. The Bulls, on the other hand, had plenty of them.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Texans WR Tank Dell hospitalized after severe knee injury on TD catch vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans wide receiver Tank Dell was taken to the University of Kansas Medical Center and the team said he would remain overnight after sustaining a severe left knee injury while making a 30-yard touchdown catch in a 27-19 loss to the Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Northwestern demolishes DePaul in crosstown showdown
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. (WGN) — An early blitz on the offensive end and playmaking down the stretch from Brooks Barnhizer and Nick Martinelli helped seal a convincing win for Northwestern over DePaul in the yearly crosstown showdown between the two programs.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the Yankees agree to a $12.5 million, 1-year deal, AP source says
item.description: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the New York Yankees agreed Saturday to a $12.5 million, one-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB legend, dies at 65: Reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls face the Celtics on 3-game win streak
item.description: Boston Celtics (21-6, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (13-15, ninth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: Chicago will look to keep its three-game win streak going when the Bulls take on Boston.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bay FC Treasure Island training facility lease approved by SF mayor
item.description: (BCN) --San Francisco Mayor London Breed signed legislation on Thursday that will allow the women's professional soccer team Bay Football Club, or Bay FC, to make Treasure Island their future home with a permanent training center.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Real Salt Lake's 2025 MLS season kicks off Feb. 22 with away game in San Jose
item.description: Real Salt Lake unveiled its entire 2025 MLS Season on Thursday, along with a few crucial dates such as the club's return to the CONCACAF Champions Cup.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Utah HC erases two-goal deficit to stun Vancouver
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Mikhail Sergachev scored with 12 seconds left in overtime, as the Utah Hockey Club stormed back from a 2-0 deficit in the third period to beat the Vanouver Canucks and end a five-game home losing streak, 3-2.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
